blocks.text: In 1833, nothing was faster than horse hooves. Passengers and letters depended of horses and men. And the news wasn't faster than a fast horse. But then, like today, the journalists they searched without ceasing the fastest ways to broadcast the news. "Swift and truth" was the axiom of the profession. Julius Reuter, dedicated his life to this axiom. Today the great men of journalism, still live for these 2 words: truth and speed. It's them, the men of the fourth power, what this film is dedicated to. Here it comes! - Can I help you?
- No, thank you. Can I help you? No, no, no! Okay! Welcome, sir. Could you tell me where it is? the Bender bank? - The Bender bank? - Yes. Do you see the boy with the hat?
Yes. He works there. Thanks. I've waited long enough! Can I have my mail now? - You'll have to wait. - Boy. My name is Julius Reuter. Sorry, Mr. Reuter.

Could you take me to the Bender bank? - As soon as you have my mail. - Thanks. Uncle! Uncle! The London and Paris mail is delayed because of the storms. When it's not the rain, It's the drought! There's always an excuse... to the inefficiency of our courier service! But, wait! - It's uncle!
- Silence! That knight wants to see you. P. - It's okay. - What do you want? - I want to raise money. - How much? 100 florins. Do you have a letter of credit? Yes.

I am Carew, earthly messenger from India to England. From India over land? It's faster than by sea. When I arrive in London I bring news from India at just 1 month old. When I'm luckier than this time... But I will make up the time when you arrive in Cologne. Cologne? It's shorter for Hamburg! A special train take me to Ostend, and there, I will cross the English Channel. Like a special train? That must cost a fortune!

Julius! Don't be insolent! It's okay. Look, boy. I mean, Mr. Reuters... that the Times It is the richest newspaper. Money is not a problem. The news is the thing most important in the world! Even at 1 month old? Yes, because you can't have earlier!

Very soon it will be faster! There is a teacher who calls Gauss. Sends signals via cable to your friend Weber. He says they communicate It's the speed of light. - Cable! - If it works. It will work! Julius! Focus on your work! But Professor Gauss says... But can he do what he says?

I am sure! I need to see it to believe it. Judge for yourself. Professor Gauss! You must not disturb the teacher! - He's working. - We come? Not now. There he is, with his telescope. - What's the matter?

- Hans! What does the teacher do? Look at the receiver... because your friend Weber try to send cable signals. If the signals reach here, will make you vibrate the magnetic coil and the teacher will decode the message. It works! The signal arrived perfectly. It works! I knew! You must write it in your newspaper! What should I say?

"Prof. Gauss invented a way to send signals.” When the world knows, will use this invention. And the threads will multiply! I did a lot of good coming here. - What is this? - Anything. "Reuter's pigeons deliver messages faster than those sent by stagecoach or messenger." - It's fascinating! - Indeed. I'm talking about pigeons! Is it something new?

Tell me more! Let's forget about work. There is something... Hang on! What's your name? Max! Good morning, Julius! I entrusted you with a job! I was going to put up a poster. And why not here? People should know our service.

Didn't I put it here? No, not even in front of the Chamber nor at the station. - Not even in hostels? - No. Why make things more difficult? I can't count on you? Julius... Pigeons don't fascinate me as much like you. If you help me, you can see them fly with a paid message. This would allow us to eat. This makes me hungry!

Is it useless? Go back to the office! You do it very well, Julius! Go back to the office! Send a telegram to Arnheim. There is no telegraph line. Pass through another city. There is no other line between Belgium and Germany. It's a question of life or death. An employee put a wrong label.

I sent the poison to Mrs. Magnus, and she has already left for Arnheim! The diligence must stop. Just one thing to do. Send a messenger! Quickly! Hurry, please! It will never arrive in time. - Never! - But if the horse is fast!

Impossible, due diligence It takes 6 hours in advance. When you arrive in Bremen, these people will be poisoned! Did Arnheim say? Come, I can help you. Who is it aimed at? Dr. Magnus, Arnheim hospital. The message, please! "Dear doctor, I have to..." No. This is not a letter!

"Fever medicine poisoned. Do not use." Sign, please. Is it really 200 km from here? Yes, by the road. But don't look at it like a bird. Max! Max! This is it... Why two? Three will be sent. To be sure that a pigeon will arrive.

Risks must be avoided. - And this one! Max, where are you? Max. Why not to respond? What happened? You called, didn't you? I stumbled and the door... They all flew... Was it all? Lenchen, my friend, don't disappoint me. Human lives are at stake.

Lenchen, Can I count on you. You understand! You can become a hero, or a heroine... Do you trust me, Lenchen? Of course, isn't it? There it is. Fly as fast as possible. Why so much fuss? A message, Max! Finally! Our future flies with Lenchen.

Arnheim. Your father wants you to go to the hospital. Okay, Emma. Take care of the bags. - Hi, Geller! - Hello Miss Magnus. Hurry, Heinrich! You have to send them to Brussels. These pigeons all arrived today. Business must be good!

I wish so, but they arrived together and no message. It shouldn't... trust beginners. These idiots from Brussels, they let us escape. There's another one. Lenchen! You too? Come here. What happened in Brussels? Why don't any of you Do you have a message? You have one!

Give it to me. "Doctor Magnus, Central Hospital.” - Do you have the medicine? - Yes father. I hope this eases your mind. Can I help you? No, no. Don't take useless risks. Fever is contagious. Send Lenchen with the others! I gotta go!
Read this. It could have been horrible. But... How did it come to message so fast? By Reuters. - Who? - Julius Reuter. They call it "The Pigeon Madman". Arnheim. Pigeon crazy! Hello, Adelaide!
Hello, Geller! Nice to see you. Lenchen has arrived with a message? Yes. Wonderful!! It was what we needed. - See you soon. - Where you go? Business at last they start to go. Reuters, come!

I have something to say. Be brief. He saw what happened, understands the advantage of use my pigeons. Pigeons! We did tests... You said you would tell me how to make a lot of money! It's true, but! It takes 10 hours to go from Brussels to Arnheim. I guarantee that my pigeons takes less than 2 hours. I'm a banker, not a pigeon expert! Your projects do not interest me.

You haven't he... No reasonable business can depend on pigeons. They didn't tell you of the medicine? I don't listen to more than my common sense. Don't bother me anymore and leave, or I'll throw you out on the street! Don't you know what my pigeon did faster than diligence! I don't want to hear any more of your pigeons. Outside! Pigeons! Geller, he didn't know. The greatest banker!

We could wait years for a opportunity like this and kept it secret! I thought I had sense to spread the story. Why didn't you disclose it? I couldn't. No?! Why? I promise. Who asked you this promise? I'd rather not say. It was the doctor!
He asked him to keep it secret. They make mistakes and Should I pay for them?! Where are you going, Julius? Father! Mr. Reuter is here. Good. Come in. Come in, Mr. Reuters.

Mr. Reuter, nice to see you! Sit down. What happened? Did I offend you in something? Professor Magnus, I don't like to talk bad of others, but my life It depends on my pigeons. People laugh at me, but I didn't give up. Finally I would have been able to prove that my pigeons were useful. But you denied me this opportunity. I?

As? - Don't you know? - No. Mr. Reuter talks about something of which I am responsible. - You? - Yes. I asked Geller to keep it secret. Sorry, I didn't know that would harm him. That doesn't help me, Miss Magnus.

My business counts more to me than your secrets. It's not an intimate secret. Try to understand. We save lives but progress is slow. Some people still see us like sorcerers, and prefer to cure themselves with superstitions instead of science. What does it have to do with me? If this is known, all deaths from fever will be attributed to poison, even the ones that happened a long time ago. My father and his colleagues they would pass for murderers. We couldn't go on our investigations and the progress made they would be lost. I'm sorry, but I think that my daughter is right.

Saving lives is more important than business. But deciding to tell this story There's nothing we can do. Don't worry. I'm not angry anymore. When I'm not angry I can see other people's problems. Good days. It's not fair to Reuter, Aren't you dad? What can we do? My Friend, I'm afraid... I owe you an apology for your patience and help. Reuter, won't you think about giving up?
To give up? It would be very difficult. But I know I lost. Lost! In between. O Miss Magnus! What a surprise! Most of all, in this weather. I like rain. But it rains a lot, and stopped here to dry.

Sit down... near the fire. It's a good day to sitting by the fire, isn't it? And it's time to drink coffee. P. Stay, I'd like to talk with you. I have a lot of work. Mr. Reuter, I came, because I'm worried with your visit to my father. Maybe speaking... Let's find a solution. As?
We could find a solution... that will help you. Help me find my slippers. Slippers? I am really sorry. Thanks. Mr. Reuters. Don't hate us. Hate you? But why?

They did well. I seem worried, because I have things to think about. I don't renounce my pigeons, and... As? - What is it? - A pigeon. I must go and see. No, stay. I go. Will this carry a message? And if you bring it?
Mr. Reuter, can I go and see the dovecote? Yes, if you want. Thanks. Lenchen! That bird! He has the heart of a lion. So this is how they are sent the messages? Yes. Come, Lenchen, you must be hungry.

Yes, in this little bag. It's very well folded, but see, opens up to get a large piece of paper. See? Here is the message. More stock quotes! Look the other way. Certainly a word from Max. What counts, then? Russia's intervention Hungary has just been confirmed. Russia intervenes to restore peace in Hungary and maintain order in Poland.

This can create difficulties in Prussia! If Poland revolts, That would be the crisis in Russia, in England, in France. Is there a risk of provoking a war? We could get there, but anyway, the stock market will sink tomorrow. It has just been confirmed. We won't know anything in Arnheim until tomorrow. If the bankers listened to me, would sell before tomorrow. But they won't do anything. You are safe of this news? My agent never made a mistake.
That might interest to my father. - Goodbye, Mr. Reuter. - Goodbye. Goodbye, Mrs. Magnus. Goodbye, Mr. Geller. Until the evening? Of course!

How is it, Professor Magnus? Mr Bauer, I was looking for him. Did you sell my shares like I asked? Yes, but I don't understand why. Russia will invade Hungary. Ah, these are rumors for weaken the Stock Exchange! Absolutely. It has been confirmed. - Where? - In Brussels.

In Brussels! Today! And how did it arrive the news? - By heaven. - The sky? This is stupid! Rumors like that can cost you dearly. The news arrived here thanks to a pigeon. A Reuter's pigeon. Now I understand!

What does this strange man do? with Ida? Strange man? It's my friend, Julius Reuter. Why strange"? Because it doesn't have a look prosperous... But after all! Exactly! After all! I didn't behave very badly, why not? It's okay.
You'll do better next time. I hope so. My daughter. Mr. Stein, Mr. Frank, Mr. Von Constad, and Mr. Bauer, here is Mr. Reuter. I already know Mr.

Reuter. M. Reuter, wants to convince us this nonsense brought by a pigeon for some reason invented by you? Mr. Bauer, I can tolerate lack of respect for my desire to innovate, but not for my integrity. I hope your apology after reading the morning newspapers. French troops are At the gates of Rome. Brussels confirms the Russian intervention in Hungary. The Hungarians are at their limit and ponder the uprising. The Grand Duke of Posen threatens to take sides in this movement.

Now, they will believe in you. Those fools! Perhaps. I don't know. People take a while to see what goes on in front of their noses. In between. Beautiful day after yesterday's storm! Yes, beautiful day! How about it, Geller? Very good, Mr.
Bauer. Exceptionally well. Great. P. Yes? I know how to recognize my mistakes. You were right and Dr. Magnus made a good profit. I'm afraid I misjudged him. After all, making mistakes is human.

Yes? Good. Anyway, sorry. Now, let's talk business. It says that pigeons are faster than a messenger. I guarantee. But your service depends on the electric telegraph. Soon we will have agents in every capital of Europe. They will telegraph the quotations at the nearest terminal. Arnheim is the most close to Eastern Europe.

Brussels is the closest to the West. My pigeons will gather both terminals. But the telegraph It's very expensive. Only the big banks have the means to use it. That's what makes my idea original. A telegram will be sent to each agent. The cost will be shared between bankers and brokers, and thus it will be more economical. You have a practical spirit, not like me. I accept. You will be the first to sign.

Please. I will sign, but with one condition. Yes? I will pay for exclusive use of your service. - Exclusive? - Absolutely. Impossible! Think about it, Reuters! With this advantage, we will make a fortune. Thanks for coming, Mr.

Bauer. What's wrong with you? Your proposal doesn't help me interest. is smarter than I thought. Gave him part of my profits. No. What if we join together? Mr. Bauer, If I explore this idea, I will have all the profits. Without sharing them with you.

But I have other ideas. I want to build my future at the service of others. And that too could help you. - Hello Bauer. - How are you, Constadt? Constadt, please. Mr. Reuter, may I have the honor of Be your first user? Come, gentlemen. Mr.
Reuter will receive it. Geller! - How is Mr. Reuter here? - Yes, Miss Magnus. Sorry, but only subscribers can enter. Me neither? Mr. Reuters said no exceptions. Business is business.

Knights, the first pigeon You should arrive at 1:30 pm. Since your arrival, the door will be locked. When you finish copying the numbers, the door will be opened. Closed? So that each one be treated equally. If you think this is unfair, he can go. Sit down, please. Only 15 minutes left to wait. I still don't believe it. It's 13:15 and you he promised us Paris, Brussels quotes and London.

Let's see if he fulfills his promise. It's here! Geller, open the door! Miss Magnus. I was prevented from entering, so I came up here. Was it you who rang the doorbell? Yes. I knew you would come. I'm going to Brussels. I would like to see you before leaving.

Why? To wish you good luck. Thank you Miss Magnus. If I fail, It won't be your fault. You forgive me this false alarm? Of course, but not the subscribers down there. - I'll warn them. - Goodbye. No, no, please. You can't see it.

I would prefer you to stay. I'll stay as long as I can. - Oh, gentlemen! - So? It was a false alarm. He said they would arrive in minutes! I said at 1:30. It's already... Why don't they arrive? Come. What if Max let me down?
- What was the scheduled time? - 13:30. It's almost 2pm. Julius! Julius! What it was? - They're leaving! - As? - Oh, wait! - The pigeons will come.

- I've waited too long. A little patience! I've had a lot of patience. There they are! Close the door, Geller! 3% loan 82.40 francs, 5%, 117.40 francs. They will have London quotes at 16:00. Did you write everything down? Open the door, Geller. Julius.

For success of your carrier pigeons! Must be the man happier. Max. My best friend and my worst enemy. But, Julius, Maybe it wasn't your fault. The quotes were incomplete. Nobody noticed. Except me! We need to be trustworthy. I don't know what to do.

I must stay here, but also go to Brussels to talk to Max. I could do it! You? Yes. I didn't want to bother her. But I'm going to Brussels! I must write a letter. Death threats and destruction. On the contrary, I will tell you that breaks my heart. Poor Max, we have to treat him like a child!

Write the letter, and deliver it to me before my departure. Almost forget! I did something for you. For me? Yes. A little bag. For your pigeons to carry messages. But, I don't deserve it... Now, finish the letter... before leaving the stagecoach. Bon voyage! Thank you, Mr.
Geller. I must say... I don't know what she sees in you. What murmurs? She likes you. You're crazy. For God, What could that beautiful girl do? to see in me? - Exactly. - Clear. What?

Nonsense! Gave me one explosive message. "Even a saint I would lose patience with you." What violence, huh? I thought you were friends with Mr. Reuter. Since always! You should be ashamed. I suppose I should. You should pay attention to these signs! They're good, aren't they?

Excellent. Mr Reuter did not put them there to ignore them. No, I made them. Look: "Reuter, the king of speed." If you have written this, be up to par. Does an engineer have to sleep in the tunnel he built? It's not funny. Sorry. Making slogans I forgot about business. - Give me a broom. - What are you going to do?

I'm going to clean this trash can. Where can I find a broom? Find one! Don't throw away my poems! These are not poems. No, this is a message to Reuters. Poor Julius Reuter! What time do pigeons come out? To arrive on time. - About... - Be precise.

When exactly? There it is! Close the door. It's catching wind, Mr. Bauer. Do I close the window? No thanks, I prefer some of fresh air. Well, as you wish. Max is perfect. I did well to write the letter.

What happened? Bauer's employee It's here too. What are you going to do? Teach Bauer a lesson. Gentlemen, let's start with London. These quotes are flying high, my friends pigeons will lose sight of them! Stop the jokes! Give us the numbers. Consols: 78.5. Funding Loan: 113 et 4.91%.

Great Western Railway: 2.56. Liverpool and Birkenhear: 9.05. That's all London. Attention, I made a mistake. I'll say the numbers again. Great Western: 2.47. And Liverpool Birkenhead: 89. What a fall! More than 3 points. Stein, write down the numbers for me.

- Open it. - No, I'm sorry. I must leave immediately. No, no, you can't. Mr. Reuters. Tell him to let me out! Of course, if gentlemen are in agreement. Here are the numbers for Paris. Charleroi mining company: 169.75 francs.

Shares of St. Tienne: 278 francs. Orleans Railway Company: 11.84; 3% slope... To the bank! Quickly! Quickly! Did you see his face? This will teach you not to overtake others. Professor Magnus! Are you okay? - Did you come from Brussels?

- Yes. Did you have a good trip? Like always. There were interesting people in diligence? Always the same. Didn't you feel lonely? Why? Sometimes, when you travel alone... No, not my daughter came with me. I had a job. It's a shame.

That tell you that my letter for Max it worked. Really? Oh yeah. Messages arrive punctual 2 weeks ago. She will be happy to hear that, because she runs her office in Brussels. But it's impossible! I also thought, before seeing her work. I didn't know, Prof. Magnus. This is terrible!

What must you think of me! That's lucky. But I'm not impartial. Write to him to thank him. I knew she was crazy for you! She has a quick one. The bag she made. Fly as fast as possible. Put them in next due diligence. Why do you send them?

to Arnheim? Why don't they fly? When it comes loose a carrier pigeon, always returns to your home. And these pigeons' home is here. I already understand! Max! Max! Did a pigeon arrive? Silence! I'm in the middle of composing a poem!

It's desperate! The answer to the question that I'm afraid to do Is it yes or no? It's no good! It's better to help her! Goodbye poetry! I promise I will dedicate myself to the pigeons! Hurry, Lenchen, He's waiting! Since he never rests, brought the food. - I'm not hungry. - With the.

I watch. Here it comes! May I congratulate you? YES How did you get it? The hole between Arnheim and Brussels is closed! The lines will work in a month. Time passes quickly when the outcome is uncertain. It's not fair. After everything you've done. Despite prejudices, the obstacles... In the end you had won, and now this... Lenchen!

What happened? I found her on the road killed by a line by telegraph! Before, there was nothing there. One of the workers saw everything. She has a broken neck! Back to work, gentlemen. Crying is no use, dear. But Lenchen... Built our future, wasn't it? And now, she's dead. The pigeon mail died with him.

It's progress, baby. No one can oppose progress. Let this be his epitaph: "Victim of progress". The progress will not destroy us? Of course not. Destroyed my business, but at the same time, makes the world smaller. I believe in progress. How do I believe in a world what do men are closer. The goal of progress It is knowledge. And through him, the truth!

Ah, Max, we're not getting anywhere! Right. We should be happy for earning a living. But when I go to Paris, I can't take my wife! There are many men strolling, except me! Ida is valid for all women! Julius, we're in Paris! Stop praising your wife. My friends wouldn't understand it. You should be ashamed of your friends!

I can't offer anything else with my salary! I'm a director, employee, everything! Ida is not in charge of the London office? She has you. You're right, Max. There they are! Yvonne, how about it? Jeanette! Let me introduce you to my friend, Mr. Reuter.

The news is not very encouraging, right? War, always war! During my life, it has always been like that. And I'm already old! You have seen many changes! Yes sir. Lots of. But most of all, the world advances faster and faster. Everything arrives so quickly! My newspapers don't have more than a day late and they don't tell us anything.

Sevastopol was taken, our troops are in Crimea willing to kill. We have to read what is hidden. See here below. Are you here? "There will be a meeting between the Allied General Staff and the Russian generals." Is it a sign of peace? You'll know in a week. Why? Louis Napoleon will speak to deputies. He will announce peace himself. The whole world waits this speech!
The whole world... Why does Paris always do Tremble, my heart? Excuse me! What's up? I'm an idiot! The biggest! - It's incredible!!! - Sit down. - Let's go! - I can't go. One moment!
- Will you come back? - Wait for me. What's up? Got crazy? Worse! I'm an idiot! You already said it. You don't understand, Max. - I should have thought years ago. - What?

The news! It's the most important thing in the world! With the telegraph, the words they fly at the speed of light. What does it have to do with you? Precisely. Every city in Europe It is served by the telegraph. Why not broadcast the news as far as messages personal? How would you get all the news? We have correspondents all over Europe, don't we? What about?

They are not reporters. They are in the heart of an organization. You could go visit them and check your competence. We need good reporters. - Let's start soon. - One moment! It's a radical idea. The telegraph works since years ago. But no one uses it for the latest news! There's a reason for that.

Nobody ever thought about that. Max, let's telegraph news! The world will be up to date fast enough. Allow me not be in agreement. London will be informed 24 hours later! Without giving advice to His Majesty, the best thing would be for you to know The English react quickly! What do you propose? I will reserve the telegraph between here and London. Telegraph the speech to London? Yes sir.

No one will stop you, once the speech has been delivered. Sir. Yes but... It will be too late, sir. Time will be wasted translating the speech into English. Sorry, there is no other solution. Yes, there is, sir. I need to have the speech before. - This has never been done. - Naturally. And no speech It was never telegraphed.

One innovation creates another. It's progress. The world must know quickly what the leaders say, how they decide their destiny. And the leaders will know right away people's opinions. I would like to create a new genre of journalism. And they will know that Louis Napoleon participated in this creation. Excellent idea. His majesty! I would like to know more about his "new journalism". Follow me.
The hotel. Yes sir. So? - They agree. - Formidable! On one condition. Let us wait for beginning of the speech. - Will we have enough time? - If you book the telegraph now. - Who?

I? - Yes, you. Nonetheless, I will prepare everything in London. Something else? Lord Palmerston canceled your appointment. The man thinks he is more important than the Times of London? You will regret it. Anything else? Mr. Reuter is waiting for you.
I don't have time. He insists and waits for you. I will be back in an hour. Mr. Delane? - It must be Rooter. - Julius Reuter. Forgive me, sir. I have an important appointment. You know my proposal.

- But.... - Let me talk. The Times already has reporters who write the news. But that costs a fortune. You're wasting your time, it's mine. The Times will not run this risk. Try one month for free. The Times has its ways, Mr. Rooter. - Reuter, Reuter!

- And more, the idea of ​​a news agency It's absurd. We want exclusivity to be the first! This is journalism! The news must be fresh. Even a newspaper like the Times cannot support exclusive use of the telegraph. If you use... I understand. But we already have one service abroad. Which costs £40 a month. I propose for 30 pounds. It's a huge risk.

Try one month for free. Free? I just ask that you use the name Reuter, And use your name as the first customer. That seems fair. God bless the Scots! The Advertiser was the first. Then it was the Post, the Herald and the News. - The Times? - Not yet. Here's the whole story.

It will be very expensive to rent the cable from Paris to London. We don't have that sum. The money given by newspapers help us. - We won't have anything this month. - What? I offered the service free of charge for them to accept. I'm sorry, Ida. - Why? - Stay! I'll go before I lose calm and a friend.

As? - You are completely crazy. - Perhaps. Even if it covers the whole world, and it works, the Times will reject it! These newspapers accepted it for free. The Times isn't even free! Every cent you receive it will be for expenses. And God knows who received little. Your wife deserves better! Can you lend me 1000 pounds?

As? We provide you with stock quotes 6 years ago. He made a fortune, as well as others, Thanks to our reliability. For the first time, I find myself obliged asking him for something. - But... - I know what I do. Just say yes or no. - When do you need it? - Tomorrow. You will have the money! Thank you, Sir Randolph.

I won't forget him. Sir Randolph doesn't like it of the idea? I don't think so. That won't come. You will have to ask for a loan. I already did. How did you borrow it? Dear, Let's take a big risk. It's all or nothing. You believe in this idea.

The world is still very big. Let's make it smaller. And now, since the news are under our dominion, we have to include "news" in the name of our company. Yes, Reuters Agency or something similar. Or something like that. The director of the Reuters Agency, or whatever name you choose, must be aware current affairs, right? As much as possible. Here is the first task to perpetuate in our records. This is nothing exceptional, but it will always a good effect. What?

Know... from a safe source... that Julius Reuter, president and founder from the Reuters Agency... Yes? will be a father. I? Father? The Reuter agency is always reliable. Ida, it's wonderful! It's the most beautiful news of the world! - What are you going to do? - I'll wire Max. The business is different now.

We cannot allow take risks now. Not with a baby. From now on, the baby is the most important thing. It will be exactly as planned. We will go on the adventure together. The three. Gentlemen Senators and deputies! In our last interview, the circumstances were complex. The allied troops were being closed with an obstinacy that left glimpse doubts in his final victory. A message to the Reuters Agency from London.

I was waiting for you. I hope too let the emperor begin. The emperor! That's why it was rented the cable for an hour! The Mourning Herald! The proclamation of peace! Special edition! I need to write to my father... and Geller too! I wish they were here. Julius!

It is a triumph that transforms journalism! It's a revolution for the written press Let us not exaggerate, my friend. This man brings the news of peace to a world at war without an "I already told you". I love him even more. The Times! Special edition! How did you get the Times of this speech? I sent it. You told me that Delane refused to subscribe. He will accept.
Not Delane. He's very stubborn. He could have been ridiculed if he hadn't... Mr. Reuters! Yes? Mr. Delane would like to see you. Delane! - Come in. - Yes sir.
I want to cry. Wait for Delane to leave, we will cry together. I leave them alone. - Sir Randolph! - How are you? Mr. Rooter... Reuter, Mr. Delane. Forgive me, Mr. Reuter.

I would like... - I want to introduce you to my wife. - My respects. - I want... - Please sit. The Times is grateful, Mr. Reuters. For the first time, our newspaper publish a news from an external company. "The emperor's speech..." "received by Reuters Agency." It's an immense honor, Mr. Delane. Come on, say it! "Delane, what an idiot you are." I deserve it!

Our agency is accurate and impartial. Only the present counts, the past is ancient history. What news bring, Mr. Delane? Mr. Reuters. You can add the Times It's your customer list. Thanks, Mr Delane! I... I'm afraid I won't be able to wait. Lenchen!

"An honor that never renounce. "Who always rejects. "Which is called by error Rooter. "Weave your skein "around the world where we inhabit. "The oceans will murmur your name. "That for the whole world, "Be a guide, a tutor. "May you be blessed "as much as Reuters as much as Rooter." Amazing! But where did you learn this? Are you good for anything? It's just a small thing... published by Gazeta of Saint-James.

All Europe speaks from you, Julius. Got your name in the newspapers more often than anyone. Baby, you recited perfectly. It's time to go to bed. Say goodnight to dad. Father. - To the bed! At your service. At your service, Mr. Reuter. He is his father's son. Julius. What a beautiful surprise! My respects, ma'am. I'll sit by the fire. It's cold tonight.
